citations.description: 67da ticket: no Gerrit change URL or vote attestation in comments; completion-verifier signed at merged_log_commit 970f3231c5ed76a2be312f8635fce72186230b21
d156 ticket: no Gerrit change URL or vote attestation in comments; completion-verifier signed at merged_log_commit 970f3231c5ed76a2be312f8635fce72186230b21

8086 ticket comment: 'Gerrit change 708 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/708), which earned Verified +1 ... plus LLM-Review +1, and is MERGED on main (commit 52d7a3dc1 lineage)' — only this child has a concrete Gerrit reference
5e64 ticket: no Gerrit change URL or vote attestation in comments; completion-verifier signed at merged_log_commit 93a6da6c9f3545d45b6513a8e83576f9045151c2
a850 ticket: no Gerrit change URL or vote attestation in comments; completion-verifier signed at merged_log_commit 52d7a3dc16810b460e49e9440f9a8093cfac111f

findings.criterion: Every child's change landed on main via Gerrit with both gate votes at +1 (visible in the change's vote history at rebar.solutions.navateam.com).
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable by rule). Only child 8086 provides a concrete Gerrit reference (change 708, Verified +1, LLM-Review +1) in a ticket comment. Children 67da, d156, 5e64, and a850 have completion-verifier signatures with merged_log_commit SHAs confirming code was landed, but no Gerrit change URLs or vote attestations appear in their ticket comments. Child 0730 is an analysis-only task with no code to land (file_impact is empty). The 'both gate votes at +1' requirement for every child cannot be independently verified from repository artifacts alone for children 67da, d156, 5e64, and a850.
findings.remediation: For each child (67da, d156, 5e64, a850), record a ticket comment citing the Gerrit change URL (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/<N>), the vote outcomes (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1), and the merge timestamp. Then tag AC2 [operator-attested] on the epic if Gerrit vote history is the intended evidence type, or ensure the evidence is linkable from the repo.

citations.description: 8086 ticket comment attests: 'make lint' + 'make typecheck' + 'make test' all pass, Gerrit change 708 Verified +1, at commit 52d7a3dc1 — but 8086 is not the final child; a850 is

a850 ticket has no comment recording make lint/typecheck/test results or CI outcome after its changes landed; its merged_log_commit is 52d7a3dc16810b460e49e9440f9a8093cfac111f (same as 8086's, which may indicate they share a landing commit, but this is not confirmed in the ticket)

findings.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass on main after the final child lands.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable by rule). Child 8086's comment attests that CI passed (Gerrit Verified +1 for change 708, covering lint/typecheck/test on GitHub Actions) at commit 52d7a3dc1. However, a850 is the final child (it comes last in the sequence 67da, d156, 8086, 5e64, 0730, a850); no attestation or CI artifact records the test suite state specifically after a850's changes landed on main. The codebase alone cannot confirm this criterion is met for the post-a850 state.
findings.remediation: Record a comment on the epic (or on a850) citing the Gerrit change URL for a850, the Verified +1 vote (which reflects CI's full build/test/lint/typecheck), and the merge timestamp. Tag this AC [operator-attested] if CI gate pass is the intended evidence form, so the criterion can be credited by attestation.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
